content.content:
# Installing PyCharm on Linux

Based on the documentation provided, here are the main installation methods for Linux:

## Using the Toolbox App (Recommended)

First, ensure required packages are installed:
`libxi6`, `libxrender1`, `libxtst6`, `mesa-utils`, `libfontconfig`, and `libgtk-3-bin`.

Then:
1. Download the tarball (.tar.gz) from the Toolbox App webpage
2. Unpack it: `tar -xvf jetbrains-toolbox-<version>.tar.gz -C <installation-directory>`
3. Navigate to the installation folder and run `./bin/jetbrains-toolbox`
4. Select PyCharm from the app interface

## Standalone Installation via Tar Archive

1. Download the tarball from the PyCharm download page
2. Unpack the file: `tar xzf pycharm-*.tar.gz -C <folder>`
3. Navigate to the bin subdirectory: `cd <folder>/pycharm-*/bin`
4. Execute PyCharm: `./pycharm`

## Snap Package Installation

Run: `sudo snap install pycharm --classic`

For EAP builds, add the `--edge` flag.

**Note:** The documentation indicates that "PyCharm installed via snap may encounter issues including performance degradation," so the Toolbox App approach is generally preferred.

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== PyCharm Installation Script ==="

# Update package manager
echo "Updating package manager..."
sudo apt-get update -qq

# Install required dependencies
echo "Installing dependencies..."
sudo apt-get install -y \
    libxi6 \
    libxrender1 \
    libxtst6 \
    mesa-utils \
    libfontconfig1 \
    libgtk-3-0 \
    openjdk-11-jdk-headless \
    wget \
    tar

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="/opt/pycharm"
echo "Creating installation directory: $INSTALL_DIR"
sudo mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download PyCharm Community Edition
echo "Downloading PyCharm Community Edition..."
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://download.jetbrains.com/python/pycharm-community-2024.3.1.tar.gz"
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/pycharm.tar.gz"

# Try to download; handle retries
if ! wget -q "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -O "$TEMP_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "Primary download failed, trying alternative..."
    # Alternative: try direct link without version
    DOWNLOAD_URL="https://www.jetbrains.com/pycharm/download/download-thanks.html?platform=linux"
    wget -q "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -O "$TEMP_FILE" 2>/dev/null || true
fi

# If still no file, use a known working URL
if [ ! -f "$TEMP_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$TEMP_FILE" ]; then
    echo "Using fallback download method..."
    DOWNLOAD_URL="https://download.jetbrains.com/python/pycharm-community-2024.1.tar.gz"
    wget "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -O "$TEMP_FILE"
fi

# Extract to installation directory
echo "Extracting PyCharm..."
sudo tar -xzf "$TEMP_FILE" -C "$INSTALL_DIR" --strip-components=1

# Clean up temp file
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"

# Create symlink for easy access
echo "Creating symlink..."
sudo ln -sf "$INSTALL_DIR/bin/pycharm.sh" /usr/local/bin/pycharm || true

# Create .desktop entry for application menu
echo "Creating desktop entry..."
sudo tee /usr/share/applications/pycharm.desktop > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name=PyCharm Community Edition
Comment=Professional IDE for Python
Exec=/opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh %f
Icon=pycharm
Categories=Development;IDE;
Terminal=false
StartupNotify=true
EOF

echo "=== PyCharm installation complete ==="
echo "To launch PyCharm, run: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"

If you need to install PyCharm on Linux, the official resources suggest downloading directly from JetBrains rather than relying on third-party package repositories.

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== PyCharm Installation Script ==="

# Install minimum required dependencies

sudo apt-get install -y \
    libxi6 \
    libxrender1 \
    libxtst6 \
    libfontconfig1 \
    libgtk-3-0 \
    openjdk-11-jdk-headless \
    curl > /dev/null 2>&1

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="/opt/pycharm"
echo "Creating installation directory..."
sudo mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download PyCharm Community Edition using curl with resume
echo "Downloading PyCharm Community Edition (this may take a few minutes)..."
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://download.jetbrains.com/python/pycharm-community-2024.1.tar.gz"
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/pycharm.tar.gz"

# Use curl for more reliable downloading
curl -L -C - "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -o "$TEMP_FILE" 2>&1 | grep -E "^  %|^Saved" || true

if [ ! -f "$TEMP_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$TEMP_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to download PyCharm"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Downloaded $(du -h "$TEMP_FILE" | cut -f1)"

# Extract to installation directory
echo "Extracting PyCharm..."
sudo tar -xzf "$TEMP_FILE" -C "$INSTALL_DIR" --strip-components=1

# Clean up temp file
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"

# Create symlink for easy access
echo "Creating symlink..."
sudo ln -sf "$INSTALL_DIR/bin/pycharm.sh" /usr/local/bin/pycharm 2>/dev/null || true

# Create .desktop entry for application menu
echo "Creating desktop entry..."
sudo mkdir -p /usr/share/applications
sudo tee /usr/share/applications/pycharm.desktop > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name=PyCharm Community Edition
Comment=Professional IDE for Python
Exec=/opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh %f
Icon=pycharm
Categories=Development;IDE;

StartupNotify=true
EOF

echo "=== PyCharm installation complete ==="
echo "Binary location: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"
echo "Launch command: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== PyCharm Installation Script ==="

# Install minimum required dependencies
echo "Installing minimal dependencies..."
sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
sudo apt-get install -y \
    libxi6 \
    libxrender1 \
    libxtst6 \
    libfontconfig1 \
    libgtk-3-0 \
    openjdk-11-jdk-headless \
    curl > /dev/null 2>&1

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="/opt/pycharm"
echo "Creating installation directory..."
sudo mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download PyCharm Community Edition using curl with resume
echo "Downloading PyCharm Community Edition (this may take a few minutes)..."
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://download.jetbrains.com/python/pycharm-community-2024.1.tar.gz"
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/pycharm.tar.gz"

# Use curl for more reliable downloading
curl -L -C - "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -o "$TEMP_FILE" 2>&1 | grep -E "^  %|^Saved" || true

if [ ! -f "$TEMP_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$TEMP_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to download PyCharm"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Downloaded $(du -h "$TEMP_FILE" | cut -f1)"

# Extract to installation directory
echo "Extracting PyCharm..."
sudo tar -xzf "$TEMP_FILE" -C "$INSTALL_DIR" --strip-components=1

# Clean up temp file
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"

# Create symlink for easy access
echo "Creating symlink..."
sudo ln -sf "$INSTALL_DIR/bin/pycharm.sh" /usr/local/bin/pycharm 2>/dev/null || true

# Create .desktop entry for application menu
echo "Creating desktop entry..."
sudo mkdir -p /usr/share/applications
sudo tee /usr/share/applications/pycharm.desktop > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name=PyCharm Community Edition
Comment=Professional IDE for Python
Exec=/opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh %f
Icon=pycharm
Categories=Development;IDE;
Terminal=false
StartupNotify=true
EOF

echo "=== PyCharm installation complete ==="
echo "Binary location: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"
echo "Launch command: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"

result.install_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "=== PyCharm Installation Script ==="

# Install minimum required dependencies
echo "Installing minimal dependencies..."
sudo apt-get update -qq > /dev/null 2>&1
sudo apt-get install -y \
    libxi6 \
    libxrender1 \
    libxtst6 \
    libfontconfig1 \
    libgtk-3-0 \
    openjdk-11-jdk-headless \
    curl > /dev/null 2>&1

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="/opt/pycharm"
echo "Creating installation directory..."
sudo mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download PyCharm Community Edition using curl with resume
echo "Downloading PyCharm Community Edition (this may take a few minutes)..."
DOWNLOAD_URL="https://download.jetbrains.com/python/pycharm-community-2024.1.tar.gz"
TEMP_FILE="/tmp/pycharm.tar.gz"

# Use curl for more reliable downloading
curl -L -C - "$DOWNLOAD_URL" -o "$TEMP_FILE" 2>&1 | grep -E "^  %|^Saved" || true

if [ ! -f "$TEMP_FILE" ] || [ ! -s "$TEMP_FILE" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to download PyCharm"
    exit 1
fi

echo "Downloaded $(du -h "$TEMP_FILE" | cut -f1)"

# Extract to installation directory
echo "Extracting PyCharm..."
sudo tar -xzf "$TEMP_FILE" -C "$INSTALL_DIR" --strip-components=1

# Clean up temp file
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"

# Create symlink for easy access
echo "Creating symlink..."
sudo ln -sf "$INSTALL_DIR/bin/pycharm.sh" /usr/local/bin/pycharm 2>/dev/null || true

# Create .desktop entry for application menu
echo "Creating desktop entry..."
sudo mkdir -p /usr/share/applications
sudo tee /usr/share/applications/pycharm.desktop > /dev/null <<'EOF'
[Desktop Entry]
Type=Application
Name=PyCharm Community Edition
Comment=Professional IDE for Python
Exec=/opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh %f
Icon=pycharm
Categories=Development;IDE;
Terminal=false
StartupNotify=true
EOF

echo "=== PyCharm installation complete ==="
echo "Binary location: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"
echo "Launch command: /opt/pycharm/bin/pycharm.sh"
